extraction.one_line_summary: STUYVEN Jasper won from a select front group, outsprinting DE BUYST Jasper after LAMPAERT attacked with 2km remaining but couldn't drop him
tactical_patterns: Strong group split formed after second Leberg passage, exploiting wind before traditional finale climbs decided the race.
VAN AVERMAET Greg and VAN AERT Wout missing the decisive selection shows even favorites get caught out early.
LAMPAERT's 2km attack on Bosberg failed because STUYVEN stayed in draft position, revealing sprint>late attack in two-up finale.
Quick-step DE KLERK sitting in front group while leaders chased behind demonstrates value of numbers over marquee names.

rider_signals.STUYVEN Jasper: positive: relaxed season approach yielded career-best win, showed superior positioning instinct over LAMPAERT in final 2km
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: first race since Tour de France crash, looked decent on cobbles but missed crucial wind split
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: negative: two-time winner chasing behind decisive group suggests declining ability to read early race splits
rider_signals.LAMPAERT Jasper: neutral: strong enough to make front group and attack late, but tactical error leading out sprint
rider_signals.HAUSSLER Heinrich: neutral: bridged to early move but lacked conviction to maintain effort in headwind section
rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: negative: desperately chasing alone before final climb instead of being in winning move shows CCC tactical isolation
